title: Ashveil
description: The ruins of an ancient city, destroyed in an event known only as The Burning. The stones are still black with soot after two centuries. No one settles here — locals say the ash shifts on windless nights. Scholars believe it was once larger than Vareth.

title: Drenford
description: A mid-sized town where the West River is narrow enough to ford. Famous for its bridge market — travelers between north and south stop here to trade. Nominally part of the Free Coast but too small to matter in politics.

title: The Grey Keep
description: A solitary fortress on the northwestern cliffs, built before recorded history. House Voss uses it as a military outpost and prison. The stones are a pale grey that turns almost white in fog.

title: Port Maren
description: The largest city on the southern coast. A chaotic, vibrant port where three languages are spoken daily. The Free Coast alliance maintains its loose capital here. Heavily defended against sea raiders.

title: Solmark
description: A prosperous trading city on the eastern shore of the Veiled Sea. Governed by a council of merchant houses — no single ruler. Ships from the southern coasts bring silk, spice, and contraband. The Compact was born here.

title: Thornwood Watch
description: A lone watchtower on the edge of the Thornwood forest, staffed by Northern Reach scouts. The forest below it is ancient and largely unmapped. The Watch has been continuously occupied since Year 60.

title: Vareth
description: The largest fortified city in the Northern Reach, built where the Var River meets the foothills of the Ashspine. Ruled by House Voss for six generations. Its iron gates have never been breached in recorded history. The city smells of coal smoke and river mist.

title: Veiled Shore
description: A small fishing village on the southern shore of the Veiled Sea. The lake is unnaturally still here — no waves, no birds over the water. Locals harvest a salt-resistant reed that no one has found elsewhere. Some say the sea has a bottom no one has reached.
